{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query asks for the gold standard for evaluating any obstruction in the nasal pathway. Reviewing the provided documents, none explicitly mention the mirror test, butterfly test, or checking the size of nostril as the gold standard. However, Document [1] discusses Rhinomanometry, which is used to evaluate the respiratory function of the nose by measuring pressure and flow during normal respiration. Obstruction will lead to higher than expected pressures and decreased flow. This makes Rhinomanometry the most appropriate choice for evaluating nasal obstruction.", "answer_choice": "C"}
